note: FRONTIER Act (H.R. ___, discussion draft v1.5). Tier floors reflect Sec. 2(p) and 2(w): a large/very large frontier developer must clear BOTH the revenue and AI R&D floors (36-month lookback), so both floors are on with combine 'and'. The 36-month lookback itself isn't representable — firm figures are effectively point-in-time. Firm revenue/AI R&D figures match the app's default lab roster (see LAB_FIGURES in index.html).

obligations.d: Before or concurrently with deploying a new or substantially modified frontier model, publish a machine-readable report covering a contact mechanism, release date, supported languages, output modalities, intended uses, restrictions, each catastrophic-risk assessment and its results, and third-party involvement. Confidential deployments may defer publication if the report goes to the Under Secretary.
May not knowingly make an inaccurate statement or convey a false impression in a transparency report; good-faith, reasonable statements excepted.

Redactions in published reports are limited to trade secrets, risk-prevention mechanisms, cybersecurity, public safety, national security, or legal compliance; each must be described and justified, the redacted material retained 5 years, and an unredacted copy transmitted to the Under Secretary.

Report a critical safety incident (weight exfiltration, materialized catastrophic risk, loss of control, deceptive subversion of controls) to the Under Secretary within 72 hours of reasonable belief; within 24 hours to law enforcement if it poses imminent risk of death or serious physical injury.

The Secretary of Commerce may issue provisional (45-day) and final (90-day, renewable) orders suspending or restricting development, deployment, or internal use of a model presenting imminent catastrophic risk; violations carry civil penalties up to $10M per violation per day and criminal penalties up to $1M and 10 years for willful violations.

Violations of the transparency and reporting title are subject to civil penalties up to $1M per violation, each day a separate violation, enforceable by the U.S. Attorney General and opted-in State Attorneys General.

Write, implement, comply with, and publish a frontier AI framework covering standards incorporation, catastrophic-risk thresholds and assessments, deployment and internal-use decisions, third-party assessment, framework updating, model-weight cybersecurity, incident response, and internal governance; must meet the Under Secretary's minimum framework regulations.

obligations.t: Annual framework review and update justification
obligations.d: Review the framework at least annually, republish if updated, and publish a justification within 30 days of any material modification.

Retain an independent third party annually (no cross financial interest, no results-contingent pay) to audit framework compliance with full access; publish a redacted report and high-level summary within 30 days and transmit copies to the Under Secretary and Attorney General; retain unredacted report while any model is deployed plus 5 years.

Submit through the Under Secretary's confidential mechanism, at least every 3 months, a summary of assessments of catastrophic risks arising from internal use of frontier models, including internally deployed models.

May not develop, deploy, or operate a frontier model without a current disclosure statement filed with the Under Secretary and fees paid; statement identifies the developer, offices, 5%+ beneficial owners (50%+ if publicly traded), and three points of contact; renewed annually or upon ownership transfer or material change; $10,000-per-day penalty for noncompliance.

Retain an independent verification organization licensed by the Under Secretary for ongoing assessment of the adequacy of the frontier AI framework, governance, risk-monitoring, and mitigations for achieving acceptable levels of catastrophic risk mitigation, covering internal use as well as third-party release.

Grant the IVO timely access on request to unredacted materials, records, personnel, systems, and other information reasonably necessary; security protocols must be narrowly tailored to protect trade secrets and confidential business information, and any material limitation is described in the assessment report.

IVO submits assessment reports at least every 6 months to the developer and Under Secretary simultaneously; within 14 days the developer describes corrective actions to the IVO, the IVO opines on adequacy within 7 days, and within 30 days the developer files a post-assessment report with the Under Secretary including key findings and the IVO opinion.

When the IVO issues a supplemental report (prior findings invalid, corrective actions inadequate, or prior representations misleading), respond to the IVO within 7 days with corrective actions and submit the response plus the IVO's opinion to the Under Secretary within 21 days.

Obtain additional assessments whenever the Under Secretary requires them to reproduce or validate findings, evaluate corrective-action implementation, or monitor significant risk changes, including after a critical safety incident or substantial modification.

Within 30 days of receiving an assessment report, publish a high-level summary and a redacted copy, and transmit the redacted report to the Under Secretary, Attorney General, and opted-in State Attorneys General; redactions are subject to overbreadth review by the Under Secretary.

Retain unredacted assessment reports and supporting materials for at least 5 years after each assessment, and provide the Under Secretary and Attorney General access to unredacted reports and materials on request.

Violations of the assessment, access, reporting, disclosure, or opt-in provisions, or material misrepresentations in a post-assessment report, are subject to civil penalties up to $1M per violation, each day a separate violation; IVOs may refer violations for enforcement and must refer imminent catastrophic risks to the Secretary within 72 hours.
